title: The Godly Paradox: Hard on Himself, Gentle with Everyone Else
caption:
Most men run this exactly backward. Hard on the people around them, endlessly patient with their own sin.

Check where your scrutiny actually goes. Give away the patience you keep rationing for yourself. Save the anger for your own compromise, not for someone else's mistake. Hand out grace before you're asked for it.

Jesus wept over a city. He also flipped tables in a temple. Both were real. He never turned that fire on the broken people who came to him. He saved it for what was rotting the house of God.

"Be kind to one another, tenderhearted, forgiving one another, as God in Christ forgave you." — Ephesians 4:32

A man who remembers what he was forgiven for stops making people earn his patience.

Save this for the next time your judgment starts pointing the wrong direction.

#faith #christianmen #grace #godlymen #manofgod #ephesians #scripture #character
theme: He's hard on himself, gentle with others.
